behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and preserves source meaning (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real neural MT output quality (fluency/adequacy), which is non-deterministic model behavior. A stub would hide the exact failure this behavior targets (bad but syntactically plausible or overly literal translations). Rejected Workflow because behavior text describes a single model judgment step: "The primary translator produces..." with no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a fixed bilingual eval set through the real translation API; score adequacy/fluency against reference translations (or human rubric) and fail when meaning preservation/coherence drops below threshold, including cases that are literal but semantically wrong.

behaviors.description: On API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator and does not error to caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real external HTTP contract handling plus fallback behavior under actual 503 responses. In-process mocks can miss real status/transport handling differences at the network boundary (MOCK-HIDE risk). Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about error-path orchestration, not model output quality: "returns a 503 error... falls back... without returning an error." Rejected Workflow because only one decision path, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real HTTP test double/server that returns 503 to the primary API call, invoke the service through its normal interface, assert successful response with dictionary translation and no propagated error.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: This targets module handoff between fallback translator logic and SQLite repository state. Unit is too low because persistence wiring/query mapping is part of the failure mode. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real network/subprocess required; no AI-output dependence; no multi-agentic chain. Rejected System because behavior text is local DB lookup only: "stored in a local SQLite database."
behaviors.test_description: Seed a temporary SQLite DB with known phrase->translation pairs, run fallback translator end-to-end in-process, and assert exact returned translations for seeded phrases.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: Behavior includes both computation and persistence ("computes... and stores"), so cross-module wiring/state write must be validated. A pure Unit test would miss storage handoff failures. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no dependence on real model judgment if deterministic text fixtures are used, no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected System because real external infra is not required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Provide deterministic primary/fallback strings with known character-level edit distance, execute quality-tracking path, and assert stored metric value in the local persistence layer matches expected distance.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison in background.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-handoff behavior (selection precedence + async logging), not translation-quality judgment. Deterministic stubs for translator outputs are sufficient to expose routing/background-write bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory real OS/network/subprocess if run in-process; no correctness dependence on model output content; no multi-agentic sequence. Rejected Agentic due disqualifying behavior text: it requires "returns the primary translation and logs..." rather than validating semantic quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with both translator modules active (stubbed deterministic outputs), assert response uses primary output immediately, and verify background quality log entry is eventually persisted with both outputs compared.
